requirements.text: WHEN a ticket has files matching '{type}.md' only with no dot-variants, the system shall display a single '{type}' tab
WHEN a ticket has files matching '{type}.{semantic}.md', the system shall group them under a '{type}' namespace tab with sub-tabs for each semantic part sorted alphanumerically
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 2: Success Conditions, Namespace Rules

requirements.text: WHEN no '{type}.md' root file exists for dot-notation files, the system shall NOT display a '[main]' tab
WHEN a file matches 'a.b.c.md' pattern, the system shall display it as namespace 'a' with sub-key 'b.c' preserving multiple dots
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 2: Success Conditions, Namespace Rules

requirements.text: WHEN user selects a namespace tab, the system shall display the first sub-document content
WHEN sub-document files are added or removed via file system, the system shall update the tab structure via SSE within 1 second
WHEN both folder-based and dot-notation documents exist in the same ticket, the system shall display both without conflicts
Namespace parsing shall complete in less than 10ms per ticket
Tab rendering shall use existing shadcn Tabs component patterns
No layout shift shall occur when switching between namespace levels
API response shall be backward compatible - dot-notation files appear as virtual folders in existing subdocuments array
IF a file matches 'a.b.c.d.md' with 4+ segments, THEN the system shall display namespace 'a' with sub-key 'b.c.d' preserving all dots after the first
IF both 'architecture/' folder AND 'architecture.x.md' file exist, THEN the system shall display both as separate entries without conflicts
IF a semantic part contains special characters like 'tests.e2e-smoke.md', THEN the system shall preserve the full semantic including hyphens
WHEN user navigates to a URL with format '/prj/{code}/ticket/{ticket}/{type}.md', the system shall display the '{type}' tab
WHEN user navigates to a URL with format '/prj/{code}/ticket/{ticket}/{type}.{semantic}.md', the system shall display the '{type}' namespace tab with '{semantic}' subtab
WHEN user navigates to a URL with format '/prj/{code}/ticket/{ticket}/{type}/{subfile}.md', the system shall display the '{type}' namespace tab with '/{subfile}' subtab (gray slash prefix)

WHEN user navigates to a sub-document, the system shall update the URL with the file path format '/prj/{code}/ticket/{ticket}/{type}.{semantic}.md' or '/prj/{code}/ticket/{ticket}/{type}/{subfile}.md'
WHEN viewing any ticket, the system SHALL always display a [Main] tab as the first tab in the tab bar
WHEN dot-notation files exist, the system SHALL display them ONLY as sub-tabs within their virtual folder
WHEN clicking a virtual folder tab, the system SHALL navigate to the root file (namespace.md)
WHEN clicking [main] child in virtual folder, the path SHALL be just the namespace
The API SHALL include filePath for ALL subdocument nodes. Virtual folder [main] child maps to root file (bdd.md).
URL paths: dot notation for virtual folders (bdd.trace.md), slash notation for physical folders (bdd/legacy.md)
WHEN a namespace has sub-tabs, the system SHALL order them as: [Main] first (if root exists), then dot-notation sub-tabs alphanumerically, then folder sub-tabs (with / prefix) alphanumerically
IF a physical folder child filename contains dots (e.g., 'bdd/another.trace.md'), THEN path splitting MUST preserve dots in the filename segment (split by '/' only, not by '.')

scenarios.title: Single tab display for root document
scenarios.given: a ticket with only 'architecture.md' file (no dot-variants)
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see a single 'architecture' tab (no namespace expansion)

scenarios.title: No main tab when no root document exists
scenarios.given: a ticket with 'tests.one.md' and 'tests.two.md' but no 'tests.md'
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see '[tests >]' namespace tab with sub-tabs '[one]' and '[two]' but NO '[main]' tab

scenarios.title: Multi-dot preservation in sub-key
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see '[a >]' namespace tab with sub-tab '[b.c]' (dots preserved after first)

scenarios.title: Namespace grouping with alphanumerical sorting
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see '[architecture >]' namespace tab with sub-tabs '[main]', '[alpha]', '[beta]' sorted alphanumerically

scenarios.title: Namespace selection shows first sub-document
scenarios.then: I see the content of 'bdd.scenario-1.md' (first sub-document alphanumerically)

scenarios.title: SSE adds new sub-document tab
scenarios.given: I am viewing a ticket with 'architecture.md'
scenarios.when: a new file 'architecture.review.md' is created in the ticket directory
scenarios.then: the tab structure updates to show '[architecture >]' with '[main]' and '[review]' sub-tabs within 1 second

scenarios.title: SSE removes sub-document tab
scenarios.given: I am viewing a ticket with 'tests.one.md' and 'tests.two.md'
scenarios.when: the file 'tests.two.md' is deleted from the ticket directory
scenarios.then: the tab structure updates to show only '[tests >]' with '[one]' sub-tab within 1 second

scenarios.title: Folder and dot-notation coexistence
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see '[bdd >]' namespace tab with both '[scenario-1]' and '[/legacy]' (folder content with gray slash prefix)

scenarios.title: Special characters preserved in semantic part
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see '[tests >]' namespace tab with sub-tab '[e2e-smoke]' (hyphen preserved)

scenarios.title: Root document URL displays single tab
Dot-notation URL displays namespace subtab
scenarios.then: The system displays 'architecture' namespace tab with 'approve-it' subtab

scenarios.title: Folder subfile URL displays gray slash subtab
scenarios.given: A ticket with 'bdd/legacy.md' file in folder
scenarios.then: The system displays 'bdd' namespace tab with '/legacy' subtab (gray slash prefix)

scenarios.title: Main tab always visible for primary ticket content
scenarios.then: a [Main] tab appears as the first tab

scenarios.title: Dot-notation files appear only inside virtual folders
scenarios.then: dot-notation files appear as sub-tabs within their virtual folder, not as separate top-level tabs

scenarios.title: Clicking virtual folder navigates to root file
scenarios.when: user clicks the virtual folder tab
scenarios.then: the system navigates to the root file (namespace.md)

scenarios.title: Main child path equals namespace
scenarios.given: a virtual folder with [main] child exists
scenarios.then: the path is just the namespace (e.g., 'bdd'), not dot-notation ('bdd.main')

scenarios.title: Sub-tabs ordered: Main first, then dot-notation alpha, then folder alpha
scenarios.given: a namespace has bdd.md (root), bdd.bbb.md, bdd.aaa.md, and bdd/legacy.md

scenarios.title: Physical child with dot in filename navigates correctly
scenarios.given: a ticket with 'bdd.md' (root), 'bdd.trace.md' (virtual), and 'bdd/another.trace.md' (physical child with dot in name)
scenarios.when: I click the '[/another.trace]' sub-tab in the bdd namespace
scenarios.then: URL shows 'bdd/another.trace.md' (NOT 'main.md'), the tab is active, and content loads from the physical file

obligations.title: Backend parses dot-notation filenames into namespace/sub-key structure as virtual folders
Frontend groups subdocuments by namespace with alphanumerical sorting and conditional main tab
Frontend updates URL with namespace path format
Sse broadcasts subdocument changes within 1 second
Display both folder and dot-notation together
API response maintains backward compatibility with virtual folders
Root document URL displays single tab
Folder subfile URL displays gray slash subtab
Main tab always visible for primary ticket content
Dot-notation files appear only inside virtual folders
Clicking virtual folder navigates to root file
URL uses dot for virtual, slash for physical folders
API includes filePath for all subdocument nodes
No main tab when no root document exists
Sub-tabs ordered: Main first, then dot-notation alpha, then folder alpha

testPlans.title: Verify filePath in API response for all subdocument nodes
Verify URL format uses dot for virtual folders, slash for physical folders

tasks.title: Extend SubDocument type with isVirtual field
